name: Maple Syrup Mousse
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle gelatin over water in a double boiler set over gently simmering water; stir to dissolve.
2. Add brown sugar, yolks, and salt. Whisk until thickened and gelatin is dissolved, about 5 minutes.
3. Transfer mixture to a bowl. Strain if necessary.
4. Stir in maple syrup. Cool completely.
5. Refrigerate until just beginning to set, about 15 minutes; do not allow the mixture to gel.
6. Beat egg whites and cream of tartar until soft peaks form. Add sugar 1 tablespoon at a time and beat until stiff and shiny.
7. Gently fold in one-third of the maple syrup mixture. Fold in remaining whites.
8. Beat whipping cream until soft peaks form. Fold in remaining maple syrup mixture.
9. Spoon mousse into serving dishes or pots de crème. Refrigerate several hours or overnight.
10. Just before serving, whip remaining cream with powdered sugar and vanilla until soft peaks form. Sprinkle mousse with gingersnap crumbs.
11. Garnish with whipped cream and maple candies if desired.
